articles.title: Chael Reveals His PED Stack
articles.description: It's another edition of Ask Uncle Chael, and nothing is off limits. Chael responds to questions about Alex Pereira's move to heavyweight, whether social media should influence title shots, the changing landscape for young fighters, and the all-time fantasy matchups he'd most like to see. He also discusses Tom Aspinall and the White House card, Sean Strickland's invitation status, and why MMA opportunities are greater today than at any point in the sport's history. Plus, Chael shares stories from a very different era of MMA, reflects on the evolution of performance enhancement in combat sports, and answers whether he'd ever return to face Conor McGregor. Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Josh Hokit Is One Win Away
articles.description: Alex Pereira may have accidentally revealed the next major storyline in the heavyweight division. After suggesting Tom Aspinall might not be ready when the time comes, Pereira opened the door to a fascinating possibility: Josh Hokit. If Hokit defeats Derrick Lewis and capitalizes on the attention surrounding International Fight Week, could he suddenly find himself in the title conversation? Chael examines the paths available to Hokit, Alexander Volkov, and Sergei Pavlovich, why microphone skills still matter in MMA, and how quickly momentum can change in the heavyweight division. Plus, what happens if Tom Aspinall's return continues to be delayed? Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: The Most Effective Martial Art On An Airplane
articles.description: A viral airplane incident has reignited one of the oldest debates in combat sports: what is the most effective martial art in the real world? After a Jiu-Jitsu practitioner helped subdue a disruptive passenger on a flight, Chael Sonnen examines why certain martial arts translate so well to real-life situations and why many of the common arguments against combat sports simply don't hold up. From street fights and locker rooms to airplanes and the Octagon, Chael explains why training matters more than environment, why athleticism often goes unnoticed, and why Jiu-Jitsu remains one of the most practical fighting systems ever developed. Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Chael Doesn't Buy Anthony Joshua's MMA Talk
articles.description: Anthony Joshua says he's interested in MMA, but Chael Sonnen isn't convinced. In Chael's view, this isn't about a serious career move—it's about attention. And if one of boxing's biggest stars is looking outside his sport, maybe the bigger question is why. Chael examines boxing's recent struggles against crossover competitors, from Conor McGregor vs Floyd Mayweather to Francis Ngannou's shocking performance against Tyson Fury. Along the way, Chael explains why he believes MMA owes Anthony Joshua nothing, why the UFC isn't likely to hand him a marquee opportunity, and why boxers rarely acknowledge the realities of crossing into another combat sport. Plus, Chael reveals the one boxer he believes could legitimately make the transition. Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: June 15 Changes Everything For Tom Aspinall
articles.description: Michael Bisping and Paul Felder's discussion about Tom Aspinall's eye injury sparked a much larger question: what exactly happens after June 14? Chael examines the timeline surrounding Aspinall's absence, the interim title fight between Alex Pereira and Ciryl Gane, and why the UFC's entire strategy depends on Tom remaining unavailable until that fight takes place. If Aspinall is healthy and ready, can the UFC still justify an interim championship? And if he isn't, how long can that continue before the division moves on? Chael also discusses Bisping's own experience with serious eye injuries, Eddie Hearn's controversial advice to Aspinall, and why June 15 could become the most important day in the heavyweight division. Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: If Ilia Topuria Loses…
articles.description: Justin Gaethje made a fascinating prediction: if he beats Ilia Topuria, Ilia will never fight again. Chael Sonnen couldn't disagree more. Using a lesson Daniel Cormier shared years ago, Chael explains the rare level of stardom where winning and losing no longer determines a fighter's future. According to Chael, Ilia Topuria has already reached that level. Whether he defeats Justin Gaethje or suffers his first UFC loss, the biggest fights in the sport will still be waiting. From a potential showdown with Islam Makhachev to the long-running rivalry with Paddy Pimblett, Chael breaks down why Topuria may actually have just as many opportunities in defeat as he does in victory. Plus, why Alex Pereira's career path provides a perfect example of how a loss can sometimes create even bigger possibilities. 0:00 Justin Gaethje's prediction1:15 Would Ilia retire after a loss?3:05 Daniel Cormier's lesson on greatness5:40 The highest level in MMA7:25 Why winning and losing doesn't matter9:15 Ilia vs Paddy Pimblett11:10 The Islam Makhachev possibilities13:00 Alex Pereira's career example15:20 Why losses can create opportunities17:05 Gaethje already proved it19:15 The UFC 300 comeback21:00 Why Ilia is already a superstar22:40 Chael's final answer Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: The Biggest Fight UFC Can Make? #ufc
articles.description: Rumors are flying about Arman Tsarukyan potentially fighting for the BMF title — but Chael Sonnen doesn’t believe that’s the direction the UFC is heading. Instead, Chael thinks the promotion may already have a much bigger plan in motion: Arman Tsarukyan vs. Ilia Topuria. In this breakdown, Chael explains why Tsarukyan is exactly the kind of fighter the UFC loves working with. He’ll fight anyone, at any weight class, under any circumstances — and according to Chael, that flexibility has quietly made Arman one of the most valuable contenders in the sport. He’s reportedly the backup fighter for the White House card, currently ranked at the top of the division, and appears to be next in line for an undisputed UFC title shot. But the most interesting part? Arman’s warning to Ilia Topuria. Chael reacts to Tsarukyan essentially telling the undefeated champion to go chase Islam Makhachev now — because if Ilia fights Arman first, that undefeated record may disappear. Chael believes Tsarukyan vs. Topuria has all the ingredients to become the UFC’s biggest event of the year: undefeated hype, championship stakes, stylistic danger, and real bad blood brewing underneath the surface. For more UFC analysis, MMA news, and Chael Sonnen breakdowns, subscribe for daily combat sports content. #UFC #IliaTopuria #ArmanTsarukyan #IslamMakhachev #ChaelSonnen #MMA #BMFTitle

articles.title: Nobody Wanted This Fight With Gable
articles.description: Gable Steveson finally has an opponent for his UFC debut, but Chael Sonnen believes the matchup raises more questions than answers. If the UFC truly evaluated Gable Steveson's skill level before selecting an opponent, what does this matchup tell us about where they think he is today? More importantly, why didn't a single heavyweight campaign for the opportunity? With Gable expected to receive one of the biggest spotlights of the year, Chael argues that standing across from him could have instantly changed another fighter's career. Chael breaks down the challenge of building a superstar prospect, the flaws in MMA rankings, and why some of the sport's biggest opportunities are often ignored by the very fighters who claim to want them. Plus, a look back at Khamzat Chimaev's rapid rise and what Gable Steveson's path to the top might actually look like. 0:00 Gable Steveson's UFC opponent revealed1:25 What the matchup tells us3:20 The UFC's three options5:15 Why top heavyweights weren't interested7:05 The problem with building prospects9:30 Khamzat Chimaev's rise11:45 Rankings vs reality13:50 Why prospects get stuck15:40 The opportunity nobody wanted18:00 What's next for Gable?20:05 A major flaw in MMA22:00 Chael's final takeaway Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: I just asked Alex Pereira About UFC champ, Tom Aspinal
articles.description: Chael reveals a surprising conversation with Alex Pereira that could have major implications for the UFC heavyweight division. While most fans assume the winner of Alex Pereira vs Ciryl Gane will eventually meet Tom Aspinall, Pereira isn't convinced Tom will be available when the time comes. According to Alex, his focus isn't just on beating Ciryl Gane—it's on maintaining the active schedule that has defined his UFC career. If Tom Aspinall isn't ready when Pereira wants to fight again, what happens next? Does the UFC ask the interim champion to wait? Does Pereira defend the interim belt? Could other heavyweight contenders suddenly find themselves in the title picture? Chael explores the ripple effects of Pereira's comments, why contract obligations may matter more than fans realize, and the difficult questions the UFC could soon be forced to answer. 0:00 Alex Pereira's surprising prediction1:30 Why Tom Aspinall may not be next3:05 Pereira's three-fights-a-year mentality4:45 The conversation with Ariel Helwani6:40 Why timing matters8:12 What if Tom isn't ready?10:05 Can an interim champion defend the belt?12:25 The Jon Jones precedent14:18 Potential heavyweight contenders16:05 Volkov, Pavlovich, and other options17:50 What happens if Alex demands another fight?19:30 The UFC's looming dilemma Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Rogan Raised a Huge Pereira Concern #ufc
articles.description: A lot of fans believe fighting at welterweight gives Conor McGregor a competitive advantage — but Chael Sonnen completely rejects that argument. In this breakdown, Chael explains why cutting to lightweight is actually proof of discipline, preparation, and focus, not a disadvantage. Chael responds to claims that Conor is somehow more dangerous at 170 pounds because he doesn’t have to suffer through a difficult weight cut. He compares the logic to fighters moving between every division in MMA, including featherweight, lightweight, and flyweight. According to Chael, once both athletes enter the cage on fight night, they weigh roughly the same anyway — so where exactly is the “advantage”? He also addresses comparisons involving Max Holloway moving up in weight and explains why fans misunderstand what weight classes are really testing. Chael’s core argument is simple: harder preparation creates sharper fighters. Suggesting that less work somehow equals a better competitive position completely misses the point of elite combat sports. This clip dives deep into weight cutting, MMA conditioning, fight preparation, and the psychology behind how fans evaluate size advantages in the UFC. For more UFC analysis, Conor McGregor updates, and Chael Sonnen breakdowns, subscribe for daily MMA content. #ConorMcGregor #UFC #MaxHolloway #ChaelSonnen #MMA #Welterweight #CombatSports

articles.title: Overestimating Ilia... Or Underestimating Gaethje?
articles.description: Justin Gaethje is one of the most accomplished fighters in the sport, yet almost nobody is giving him a chance against Ilia Topuria. Why? Chael examines whether fans are overestimating Ilia Topuria, underestimating Justin Gaethje, or both. After dominant wins over Alexander Volkanovski, Charles Oliveira, and Max Holloway, Topuria has reached a level where many believe he's unbeatable. But has anyone really seen enough to make that claim? Chael breaks down Gaethje's resume, his skill set, and why only a handful of fighters have ever truly looked better than him inside the Octagon. Plus, a fascinating look at Daniel Cormier's definition of superstardom and why Ilia Topuria may have already reached the point where winning and losing no longer changes his status. Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Arman Tsarukyan Isn't Really the Backup
articles.description: Arman Tsarukyan has reportedly been named the backup fighter for the White House card, but Chael Sonnen believes that designation reveals something much bigger. If Arman is scheduled to compete at Real American Freestyle the night before, can he really serve as a traditional backup fighter for a championship main event? Chael breaks down why the UFC's approach may actually prove Dana White's confidence in the athletes selected for the historic White House event. From the original promise to only book fighters he completely trusts, to a surprising comparison with the UFC's COVID-era bubble, Chael explains why the absence of a true backup fighter may be the strongest statement Dana could make. Plus, why Chael believes Arman Tsarukyan is likely next in line for the winner of Ilia Topuria vs Justin Gaethje regardless of whether he ever gets a backup call. 0:00 Arman Tsarukyan named backup fighter1:02 Why Arman can't be a true backup2:25 The weigh-in problem3:40 Dana's original White House promise5:15 Why a backup fighter contradicts the mission7:08 The UFC COVID bubble comparison9:42 Dana White and the mask symbolism12:00 Why Chael wants Arman next13:35 The winner will likely call out Arman15:10 The real message behind no backup fighter16:30 Dana's vote of confidence Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Remember When Sergei Pavlovich Was the Boogeyman?
articles.description: Not long ago, Sergei Pavlovich was the most feared man in the UFC heavyweight division. Six straight first-round knockouts. An undefeated aura. A mystery that made him even scarier. Fans were convinced Pavlovich was the future heavyweight champion—until Tom Aspinall changed everything. Now Pavlovich is back, ranked No. 3 in the world and calling for a title shot after another huge win. But Chael sees a much bigger story developing. With Francis Ngannou gone, Jon Jones on the sidelines, Tom Aspinall injured, and Alex Pereira potentially entering the heavyweight title picture, the division suddenly has more questions than answers. If Aspinall doesn't return soon, who actually deserves the next shot? Pavlovich? Alexander Volkov? Someone else entirely? Chael revisits the rise and fall of the heavyweight "boogeyman" and explains why the UFC may be headed toward a title fight few fans are expecting. 0:00 Sergei Pavlovich is calling for a title shot0:19 When Pavlovich was the heavyweight boogeyman1:14 Why mystery helped build his reputation1:51 The backup fighter story2:08 Tom Aspinall changed everything2:52 Heavyweight's current problem3:56 Why Alex Pereira changes the equation4:31 The forgotten contender: Volkov5:08 If Pavlovich gets consideration, Volkov must too5:52 The title picture nobody expects6:33 Is Tom Aspinall's future already decided?7:08 Pereira's comments about Tom7:43 The heavyweight shuffle continues7:59 Does Pavlovich deserve the shot? articles.title: Alex Pereira Doesn't Believe Tom Aspinall is Next
articles.description: Chael just spoke directly with Alex Pereira and his coach, and the conversation raised several unexpected questions. Would an interim heavyweight title make Pereira the first-ever three-division champion? Surprisingly, Pereira doesn't see it that way. He told Chael that "champ-champ-champ" is a label for the fans to decide, not something he would claim for himself. The bigger surprise may have been what Pereira had to say about Tom Aspinall. According to Alex, he doesn't expect Aspinall to be ready anytime soon, and if Pereira wins at heavyweight, he isn't counting on Tom being his next fight. That creates a fascinating question: if not Aspinall, then what's next? Chael also reveals what Pereira's team told him about Alex's new UFC deal and why they believe the promotion can put any opponent, in any weight class, in front of him. Heavyweight, light heavyweight, interim title, undisputed title—it doesn't matter. Pereira plans to stay active. Plus, Chael explores the scenario nobody is discussing: what happens if Pereira loses? 0:00 Alex Pereira reacts to "champ-champ-champ" 0:43 Does an interim belt count?1:07 Pereira's surprising answer 1:34 Why the achievement may not matter to him 2:00 Pereira doesn't expect to fight Tom Aspinall 2:33 Aspinall's injury timeline 3:10 The interim title problem 3:47 What if Pereira loses? 4:27 Pereira's coach reveals details of new UFC deal 5:07 "We'll fight anybody" 5:45 Why Chael appreciates Pereira's approach 6:21 The biggest takeaway from the conversation Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench
